title: Auto-properties
instruction:
Auto-properties generate a backing field automatically.

Run this.
successMessage: { get; set; } is the full auto-property. It generates a private backing field transparently.

title: Read-only property
instruction:
`{ get; }` without a setter makes the property read-only after construction.

Run this.
successMessage: Read-only properties can only be set in the constructor. Area uses an expression body (=>) — a computed property.

title: get with validation in set
instruction:
Provide logic in the setter to validate before accepting values.

Run this.
successMessage: The setter validates the value before assigning. Fahrenheit is a computed property — no backing field needed.

title: init-only properties
instruction:
`init` allows setting only during construction — immutable after.

Run this.
successMessage: init-only properties enforce immutability after construction. Great for configuration and value objects.

title: Required properties (C# 11)
instruction:
`required` forces callers to initialise a property.

Run this.
successMessage: required ensures Name and Price are always set. Category has a default. Callers cannot create Product without providing the required properties.

title: Computed properties
instruction:
Properties without a setter compute their value from other properties.

Run this.
successMessage: Computed properties are recalculated every time they are accessed. They keep the class consistent — no stale cached values.

title: Private setter
instruction:
`{ get; private set; }` allows reading from outside but only setting from inside.

Run this.
successMessage: private set means only Counter's own methods can change Count. External code can read it but not write it.

title: Static properties
instruction:
Static properties belong to the class, not instances.

Run this.
successMessage: Static properties are accessed via the class name. They share state across all code — useful for configuration and singletons.
